digest.component_or_area: project landing infrastructure (Gerrit + GitHub Actions serial auto-lander)
propositions: Parallel agent sessions racing to land on a fast-forward-only main cause manual rebase-retries and redundant CI runs, wasting wall-clock and agent attention.

A serial auto-lander service should land the front Autosubmit change/chain by rebasing to tip, awaiting fresh Verified, and ancestor-atomic submitting the exact tested SHA without weakening the two-vote gate.
The agent contract is one land command returning a single typed terminal outcome (merged, needs_rebase, ci_failed, review_failed) so agents never correlate Gerrit primitives themselves.
Landing logic must stay project-specific infrastructure and add no Gerrit-specific code to platform-agnostic rebar core, deferring a provider-seam to a separate idea.
Cross-stack batching throughput was evaluated and rejected, deferring throughput to bounded speculation as a Phase-2 concern.
The delivered Phase-1 serial auto-lander was implemented, landed, ran live, then retired and superseded by a Gerrit-native Rebase-If-Necessary approach (ADR-0047), closing the epic as superseded.
